title: A 'Super' El Niño Could Make Food Prices Rise Until 2028
subtitle: Economists warn that extreme weather patterns may cause long-term global food price increases.

summary.en: A powerful weather event known as a 'super' El Niño is expected to hit the world this year. Experts warn that this could cause a major shock to global food prices. The impact of this weather pattern may last until 2028. Many countries are already dealing with high living costs due to other global conflicts. Central banks are concerned that this will keep interest rates high for a longer time.
summary.zh: 一場被稱為「超級」聖嬰現象的強大氣候事件預計將於今年席捲全球。專家警告，這可能對全球食品價格造成重大衝擊，影響恐持續至二〇二八年。許多國家已因其他全球衝突而面臨高昂的生活成本。各國央行擔心，這將導致利率在更長的時間內維持在高檔。

paragraphs.en: The world is facing a new challenge that could make the price of food rise for several years. Economists are warning that a 'super' El Niño weather cycle, which is expected to begin this year, may cause a severe shock to global food prices that could last until 2028. This weather event happens when changes in wind patterns allow warmer water to spread across the Pacific Ocean. Scientists say there is a very high chance that this event will lead to extreme heatwaves, heavy flooding, and stronger storms around the world.
paragraphs.zh: 全球正面臨一項可能導致食品價格連續數年上漲的新挑戰。經濟學家警告，預計於今年開始的「超級」聖嬰現象（El Niño）氣候週期，可能會對全球食品價格造成嚴重衝擊，影響恐持續至二〇二八年。當風向模式的改變使得較溫暖的海水擴散至太平洋時，就會發生這種氣候事件。科學家表示，此事件極有可能導致全球各地出現極端熱浪、嚴重洪災以及更強烈的風暴。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Environment, this 'super' El Niño comes at a difficult time. Many households are already struggling with high living costs. The situation is made worse by the ongoing war in Iran, which has already pushed global food prices to their highest level in three years. Experts note that the world is now facing two major problems at the same time: conflict-related supply issues and extreme weather linked to global heating.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報環境版》（The Guardian Environment）報導，這次的「超級」聖嬰現象發生在一個艱難的時刻。許多家庭已經在為高昂的生活成本所苦，而伊朗持續進行的戰爭使情況雪上加霜，已將全球食品價格推升至三年來的最高水準。專家指出，世界目前正面臨兩大問題同時夾擊：與衝突相關的供應問題，以及與全球暖化相關的極端氣候。

paragraphs.en: This is not the first time El Niño has caused problems. In the past, this natural phenomenon has led to serious issues for farmers and food supplies. More than a century ago, a very severe El Niño caused terrible droughts in many countries, including China, Brazil, Egypt, India, and parts of Africa. Today, the US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) says that the 2026-27 cycle could be even more severe than previous events. There is a 63% chance that sea temperatures will rise significantly, which would increase the risk of droughts and floods that destroy harvests.
paragraphs.zh: 這並非聖嬰現象首次引發問題。過去，這種自然現象曾對農民和糧食供應造成嚴重困擾。一個多世紀前，一次極為嚴重的聖嬰現象曾在包括中國、巴西、埃及、印度及非洲部分地區在內的許多國家引發可怕的乾旱。如今，美國國家海洋暨大氣總署（NOAA）表示，二〇二六至二〇二七年的週期可能比以往的事件更為嚴重。海面溫度顯著上升的機率高達百分之六十三，這將增加乾旱與洪災摧毀農作物的風險。

paragraphs.en: Analysts at Goldman Sachs have predicted that the strength of this El Niño could cause a 15.8% increase in global food commodity prices. This would have a 'knock-on effect'—a situation where one event causes a series of other events—around the world. For example, consumers in Europe could see food prices rise by 1.3%. However, experts say the full impact will not be felt immediately. Because of the different times it takes to grow and harvest crops, the consequences of this weather event may take until the second half of 2028 to be fully realized.
paragraphs.zh: 高盛集團（Goldman Sachs）的分析師預測，此次聖嬰現象的強度可能導致全球食品大宗商品價格上漲百分之十五點八。這將在全球引發「連鎖反應」，即由單一事件引發一連串後續效應。例如，歐洲消費者可能會面臨食品價格上漲百分之一點三的情況。然而，專家表示，完整的影響不會立即顯現。由於農作物生長與收成的時間不同，這場氣候事件的後果可能要到二〇二八年下半年才會完全體現。

paragraphs.en: There are other factors that make the situation more complicated. The movement of food around the world depends on canals and rivers. If water levels in these areas change because of the weather, it will be harder to ship food to where it is needed. Furthermore, analysts at UBS point out that El Niño does not affect agriculture in the same way everywhere. While it creates 'losers'—areas that suffer from bad weather—it might also create 'winners' in some regions where the warmer weather helps crops grow better. Despite this, the overall effect is expected to be negative for the global food supply chain.
paragraphs.zh: 還有其他因素使情況更加複雜。全球糧食的運輸仰賴運河與河流，若這些地區的水位因氣候而改變，將使糧食運往需求地的難度增加。此外，瑞銀集團（UBS）的分析師指出，聖嬰現象對各地農業的影響並不相同。雖然它會造成「輸家」——即那些遭受惡劣天氣影響的地區，但也可能在某些氣候變暖有助於作物生長的地區創造「贏家」。儘管如此，預計對全球食品供應鏈的整體影響仍將是負面的。

paragraphs.en: This situation is also worrying for central banks, which are the organizations that manage a country's money and interest rates. If food prices go up, it creates a new type of inflation—a general increase in prices and a fall in the value of money. This 'climateflation' means that central banks might need to keep interest rates at high levels to stop prices from rising too quickly. Analysts at the Italian bank UniCredit noted that recent heatwaves in Europe are a reminder that the climate is already changing. They believe that El Niño will add a new layer of pressure to an already fragile system.
paragraphs.zh: 這種情況也讓負責管理國家貨幣與利率的中央銀行感到擔憂。如果食品價格上漲，將會產生一種新型的通貨膨脹，即物價普遍上漲且貨幣價值下跌。這種「氣候通膨」意味著中央銀行可能需要維持高利率，以阻止物價過快上漲。義大利裕信銀行（UniCredit）的分析師指出，近期歐洲的熱浪提醒了世人氣候正在改變。他們認為，聖嬰現象將為本已脆弱的系統增添一層新的壓力。

paragraphs.en: In summary, the combination of war, supply chain problems, and the arrival of a 'super' El Niño creates a difficult future for global food prices. As the world waits to see how strong the weather event will be, experts are watching closely to see how it will affect the food on our tables. While some areas may be luckier than others, the global economy is preparing for a long period of uncertainty that could last for several years.
paragraphs.zh: 總結來說，戰爭、供應鏈問題以及「超級」聖嬰現象的到來，為全球食品價格的未來蒙上陰影。隨著世界等待觀察這場氣候事件的強度，專家們正密切關注它將如何影響我們餐桌上的食物。雖然某些地區可能比其他地方幸運，但全球經濟正準備迎接一段可能持續數年的長期不確定時期。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A sudden, violent, and upsetting event or change.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（震驚），這裡作名詞，指對經濟造成的劇烈影響。文中：may cause a severe shock to global food prices that could last until 2028.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An event that happens as an indirect result of another event.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是慣用語，字面意思無法直接推敲。文中：This would have a 'knock-on effect'—a situation where one event causes a series of other events—around the world.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An additional level or amount of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（實體層），這裡指抽象的壓力層次。文中：They believe that El Niño will add a new layer of pressure to an already fragile system.

sources.name: The Guardian Environment
sources.title: ‘Super’ El Niño could cause global food price shock lasting into 2028, analysts say

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the specific probability that sea temperatures will rise significantly during the upcoming 2026-27 El Niño cycle?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，美國國家海洋暨大氣總署（NOAA）明確指出，2026-27 年週期中，海水溫度顯著上升的可能性為 63%。選項 A 是高盛預測的糧食價格漲幅；選項 C 是歐洲糧食價格的預期漲幅；選項 D 則完全未在文中提及，屬於干擾項。

quiz.question: Why might the full impact of the 'super' El Niño on food prices not be felt until the second half of 2028?

options.A: Because the war in Iran will have ended by then.
options.B: Because central banks will have successfully lowered interest rates.
options.C: Because agricultural cycles and harvesting times delay the market effects.
options.D: Because the weather event will not actually begin until 2028.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第四段提到，由於農作物生長與收成需要時間，氣候事件對市場的影響不會立即顯現，因此結果可能要到 2028 年下半年才會完全體現。選項 A 文中未提及戰爭結束時間；選項 B 與文中提到的央行維持高利率以對抗通膨的預測矛盾；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中指出事件預計今年就會開始。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the term 'knock-on effect' mean?

options.A: A sudden stop in global trade.
options.B: A chain reaction where one event triggers subsequent events.
options.C: A significant reduction in agricultural costs.
options.D: A physical collision between two weather patterns.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中明確定義 'knock-on effect' 為「一個事件引發一系列其他事件的情況」，即連鎖反應。選項 A 指的是貿易停滯，與定義不符；選項 C 提到成本降低，與文中強調價格上漲的語境相反；選項 D 將該詞誤解為物理碰撞，屬於對詞彙的字面誤讀。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the upcoming 'super' El Niño?

options.A: It will benefit farmers worldwide by creating more 'winners' than 'losers'.
options.B: It is the sole reason for the current global economic instability.
options.C: It will likely cause a long-term period of uncertainty and higher food prices due to multiple compounding factors.
options.D: It is a minor weather event that central banks can easily manage.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章總結段落與全文主旨一致，強調戰爭、供應鏈問題與極端氣候（El Niño）的疊加，將導致全球糧食價格長期不穩定。選項 A 忽略了文中強調整體影響為負面的事實；選項 B 錯誤，因為文中提到戰爭也是重要因素；選項 D 與文中描述的「嚴重衝擊」與「脆弱系統」相矛盾。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
